mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.7% in Jan 2015 reflects post-crisis recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in < 55% of instances where UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains near zero, signaling sustained monetary accommodation. When FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25%, equity risk premiums compress and forward S&P 500 returns are elevated over the next 5 trading days as investors rotate into equities.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, positive 5-day returns occur in < 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with expanding housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust aggregate demand and forward economic momentum. The joint acceleration of these two demand proxies predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through improved earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1089k units in Dec 2014 shows housing recovery
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST grow month-over-month, positive 20-day returns occur in < 58% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above its 3-month moving average, signaling strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side productivity gains. This drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as profit margins expand.
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: When INDPRO exceeds its 3-month MA, positive 60-day returns occur in < 56% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 150k per month, signaling labor market cooling and reduced wage pressure. This triggers expectations of lower inflation and extended monetary accommodation, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: moderate labor market growth with contained wage inflation
failure_condition: When PAYEMS growth < 150k, positive 20-day returns occur in < 54% of instances, or the signal produces negative Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 2% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS stays near zero, creating a real negative interest rate environment. This combination incentivizes equity allocation and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors seek real returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 236.15 in Dec 2014, FEDFUNDS at 0.11%
failure_condition: When both CPIAUCSL growth < 2% and FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, positive 20-day returns occur in < 57% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized rate, signaling robust economic expansion. This triggers upward earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors extend equity duration.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth > 2.5%, positive 60-day returns occur in < 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline more than 10% month-over-month while retail sales (RSAFS) remain flat, signaling demand-side weakness and recession risk. This divergence predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reduce equity exposure.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 10% MoM while RSAFS growth < 0.5%
regime_hint: demand-side contraction with leading recession signals
failure_condition: When both conditions trigger, negative 20-day returns occur in < 55% of instances, or the interaction term fails to produce significant negative coefficient (p > 0.10).
